content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To       : File                                       Date : April 11, 1977


From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: Conversation with Anita Bobbitt from Assemblyman Tucker's
         office.

     I spoke with Anita Bobbitt of Assemblyman Tucker's office
on March 30, 1977. She indicated that Assemblyman Tucker's
controlled committee intended to buy $105 worth of fund-
raising tickets from a local candidate in the Los Angeles
elections. She asked whether the controlled committee
would be required to file campaign statements in connection
with the local election by virtue of this purchase.

     Citing 84204.1 I advised her that Assemblyman Tucker's con-
trolled committee would not be required to file campaign
statements in connection with the Los Angeles elections
by virtue of this purchase since it was less than $250.

     TP:jo

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a controlled committee must file campaign statements in connection with local elections when purchasing $105 worth of fundraising tickets from a local candidate.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No filing requirement exists under section 84204.1 because the purchase amount of $105 is less than the $250 threshold that triggers campaign statement filing obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal memorandum format rather than formal advice letter. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present. Question and conclusion extracted from narrative memo text. Reference to section 84204.1 used to support the analysis. Document is staff-to-file notation of informal advice given verbally.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a controlled committee must file campaign statements in connection with local elections when purchasing $105 worth of fundraising tickets from a local candidate.

CONCLUSION: No filing requirement exists under section 84204.1 because the purchase amount of $105 is less than the $250 threshold that triggers campaign statement filing obligations.

I spoke with Anita Bobbitt of Assemblyman Tucker's office on March 30, 1977. She indicated that Assemblyman Tucker's controlled committee intended to buy $105 worth of fund- raising tickets from a local candidate in the Los Angeles elections. She asked whether the controlled committee would be required to file campaign statements in connection with the local election by virtue of this purchase. Citing 84204.1 I advised her that Assemblyman Tucker's con- trolled committee would not be required to file campaign statements in connection with the Los Angeles elections by virtue of this purchase since it was less than $250. TP:jo
embedding.summary: FPPC staff memorandum documenting a telephone conversation with Assemblyman Tucker's office regarding whether his controlled committee's $105 purchase of fundraising tickets from a local Los Angeles candidate triggers campaign statement filing requirements. Staff concluded no filing requirement applies because the amount is below the $250 threshold.
